MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHN L. CONDREY, ESQ. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. 602-794-2460 appearing for the Plaintiffs (Plaintiff GEORGE HAUSER present);
Atty. EDWARD J. RODZEWICH, ESQ. of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel No. 510-457-3460 appearing for the Defendant 60 Rausch Street Homeowner's Association.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFFS GEORGE HAUSER, CHRISTINE HAUSER, AND RAUSCH INVESTMENT LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication, the Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. "There is a triable issue of material fact if, and only if, the evidence would allow a reasonable trier of fact to find the underlying fact in favor of the party opposing the motion in accordance with the applicable standard of proof." [See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 C4th 826, 850.] Here, the Settlement Agreement states: the HOA and Plaintiffs agree to sell eight (8) exclusive use easements for the Disputed Parking Spaces within a three year period. [UMF 3.] Plaintiffs have presented evidence that under the terms of the Settlement Agreement, Defendants must convert the disputed parking spaces non-appurtenant to Unit 404. [UMF 17.] A trier of fact could find that the Settlement Agreement allows for three years before Defendant's nonperformance constitutes a breach. Therefore, because there is a triable issue of material fact, the motion for judgment is denied. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
